observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to plan and perform the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-009 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

Relevantly, the Local Chief Executive spearheads an investigation to determine the persons accountable for the missing PPEs and demands the production of the same.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to initiate the transfer of completed infrastructure projects for use and benefit of the barangay concerned to facilitate the derecognition of the corresponding asset account in the books of the Municipality.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant consider the pertinent accounting provisions on the recognition of assets in the books with due regard to the character and nature of the transaction to prevent over or understatement in the financial statements. Moreover, make the necessary correcting entries for the above-mentioned misrecognition.
observations.reason: A list of projects located in, and intended for, the barangays, are already submitted to the office of the Sangguniang for the preparation of a resolution donating such properties, however, up to this date, it remained unacted. There was no comment given by the SB on this matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly follow the reglementary period on the submission of contracts to ensure timely review and compliance of any deficiencies that may be noted.
observations.reason: Submission of CY 2024 contracts is still delayed for an average of 35 days

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Supply Officer to gather sufficient data on the confiscated vehicle.

Relatedly, we recommend that the Supply Officer, after carefully considering the data gathered, recommend to the LCE whether to cause the recovery of the confiscated vehicle or include such in the list of properties for disposal.

Finally, the LCE to spearhead the disposal of the municipality’s unserviceable properties.
observations.reason: Reiterated in this Report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the:
The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer maintain separate records or registries for the 20% Development Fund to facilitate monitoring and reconciliation of balances and prevent any errors in the balance presented in the Statement of Comparison Budgeted and Actual Amounts concerning the said fund.
The Municipal Budget Officer to review the balances in the Statement of Appropriations, Allotments, Obligations, and Balances (SAAOB) and ensure that balances are vouched to the Record of Appropriations and Obligations (RAO).

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer remit intact the taxes withheld within the prescribed period to prevent possible lapses caused by late remittances.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant make a separate disclosure in the Notes to the Financial Statements of the exact details of the amount payable to the BIR to provide more reliable financial information to the users of the FS.

We recommended that the:
Municipal Assessor gathers data on the cost of the road lot component of each of the Local Road Network included in the Road Map of the municipality, as well as those recorded in the books of accounts, and commences the establishment of the LGU’s ownership on the lots; and,
Municipal Accountant to adjust the road network account based on the reconciled results of the data gathered from the Mun. Assessor.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Engineer, and the Property Officer identify the Road Network account and properly present the same in the preparation of the PPELC, PC, and RPCPPE-LRN.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to plan and perform the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-009 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.

We recommended that the LDRRMO and the General Services Officer:
Reconcile their respective records and identify the unaccounted fuel presented in Tables 8 and 9;
Submit monthly report on fuel consumption in accordance with the COA Circular No. 77-61, prescribing the Manual on Fuel Consumption; and,
Submit to the Audit Team a justification for the observations above.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require proper planning and identification of projects chargeable to the 20% Development Fund to ensure that all are implemented and funds utilized as intended.
We recommended that the LDRRM Officer ensures that the LDRRMF utilization is directly responsive to the purpose of the fund, taking into consideration the thematic areas of the LDRRMF.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the implementation of the Gender and Development Plan and Budget to optimize support for gender mainstreaming in the LGU.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer strictly follow the withholding of taxes on all payments to suppliers/sellers who are subjected to 1% or 2% EWT, 5% GVAT, and 3% GPT, and remit the same to BIR on time.

We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant ensure that payment for internet and other utility expenses is duly substantiated with an official receipt.

We recommended that the HRMO:
Instruct the JO Personnel to prepare their own Accomplishment Reports and conduct regular monitoring of the same;
Facilitate the execution of supplements or amendments to the original Contract of Services; and
Coordinate with department heads to assess and properly plan the needs of hiring Job Order Personnel.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive be vigilant in authorizing travel of Job Order Personnel and allow only those which are necessary for delivering the needs of the public.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall:

(a)	Trace the components of the unidentified balance of the Cash and Cash Equivalents in the Trust Fund and identify its source agencies to facilitate liquidation with its respective grantor when warranted; and

(b)	Thereafter, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statement funds received from any government agencies composing the balance of the Trust Fund as of year-end in conformity with Item 27 of IPSAS 1 on Fair Presentation and Compliance.
observations.reason: Untraceable beginning balances amount to ₱73,121.12. This was the residual amount after diligent efforts were made to trace the components of the CIB in the Trust Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments and provide a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher and Subsidiary Ledger to the audit team.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider hiring a support staff for the BAC Secretariat so that the functions and responsibilities of the Secretariat are carried out diligently.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned department heads to ensure that the 20% Development fund is well-planned and effectively utilized to cater the development needs of its constituents.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

Direct the former and the incumbent Barangay Chairperson and the Barangay Treasurer to locate the transaction documents related to the purpose of the grant and to seek alternative remedies to be able to liquidate and strictly implement proper turnover of the accounts and documents of the barangay, in as much as the transactions related to the outstanding unliquidated fund transfers are concerned, and impose appropriate administrative and legal action for refusal to do so; and,

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor and strictly implement the rules underlying all financial assistance granted to the barangay in 2021 and the years thereafter as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013 and the stipulation of the MOA.
observations.reason: The fund transfer that remains unliquidated pertains to Barangay San Isidro of which a Demand Letter was issued by the Municipal Accountant which was received by the Punong Barangay on October 17, 2024. The said demand letter was furnished to the Audit Team on October 29, 2024.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive enjoin the concerned department heads to ensure that the 20% Development fund is well-planned and effectively utilized to cater the development needs of its constituents.
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
(there are PPAs funded by EDF through supplemental budget but there are still EDF projects unimplemented as of EOY)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

Direct the former and the incumbent Barangay Chairperson and the Barangay Treasurer to locate the transaction documents related to the purpose of the grant and to seek alternative remedies to be able to liquidate and strictly implement proper turnover of the accounts and documents of the barangay, in as much as the transactions related to the outstanding unliquidated fund transfers are concerned, and impose appropriate administrative and legal action for refusal to do so; and,

Enjoin the Municipal Accountant to closely monitor and strictly implement the rules underlying all financial assistance granted to the barangay in 2021 and the years thereafter as provided for in COA Circular No. 94-013 and the stipulation of the MOA.
observations.reason: No Action on the Part of the BLGU Officials despite regular demands by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We further recommended, during the Exit Conference, that the Municipal Accountant send confirmation letters to the respective source agencies to which the funds included in the Due to NGA account belongs. This way, additional information will be available, which would aid her in establishing the composition of the CIB in the Trust Fund.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall:
Trace the components of the unidentified balance of the Cash and Cash Equivalents in the Trust Fund and identify its source agencies to facilitate liquidation with its respective grantor when warranted; and
Thereafter, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statement funds received from any government agencies composing the balance of the Trust Fund as of year-end in conformity with Item 27 of IPSAS 1 on Fair Presentation and Compliance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory Committee to plan and perform the procedures outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-009 for the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.
Relevantly, the Local Chief Executive spearheads an investigation to determine the persons accountable for the missing PPEs and demands the production of the same.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjustments and provide a copy of the Journal Entry Voucher and Subsidiary Ledger to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the General Services Officer (GSO) to initiate the transfer of completed infrastructure projects for use and benefit of the barangay concerned to facilitate the derecognition of the corresponding asset account in the books of the Municipality.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant consider the pertinent accounting provisions on the recognition of assets in the books with due regard to the character and nature of the transaction to prevent over or understatement in the financial statements. Moreover, make the necessary correcting entries for the above-mentioned misrecognition.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor consider hiring a support staff for the BAC Secretariat so that the functions and responsibilities of the Secretariat are carried out diligently.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly follow the reglementary period on the submission of contracts to ensure timely review and compliance of any deficiencies that may be noted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Supply Officer to gather sufficient data on the confiscated vehicle.
Relatedly, we recommended that the Supply Officer, after carefully considering the data gathered, recommend to the LCE whether to cause the recovery of the confiscated vehicle or include such in the list of properties for disposal.
Finally, the LCE to spearhead the disposal of the municipality’s unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer compute the area of the road lots for the entire road network system of the Municipality and provide the same to the Municipal Assessor for the latter’s use in the valuation of said lots. Furthermore, to submit the results of road lot valuation to the Municipal Accountant for recording in the books.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant presents in the Notes to Financial Statements the required disclosures for Property, Plant and Equipment as prescribed in IPSAS 17.

We recommended that the:

Supply Officer and Inspection Committee, properly accomplish the Inspection and Acceptance Report every time goods are received; and

Municipal Accountant recognize accounts payable for the unpaid purchases with properly accomplished IAR and other corroborating supporting documents.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Budget Officer review and reconcile their respective records and registries and make the necessary adjustment to correct the errors.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Engineer regularly monitors all infrastructure projects to avoid delayed implementations; and,

Municipal Accountant provides the computation of the liquidated damages for deduction from the contractor’s claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office provide the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) copies of the Drawings, Bill of Quantities, and other detailed engineering documents, as part of the Philippine Bidding Documents, for all infrastructure projects, regardless of the amount of the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor/Vice-Mayor and the Human Resources Management Officer to:

Review the support staff functions requirement per office to determine the appropriate manpower complement of said office and exercise prudence in the hiring of JO workers; 

Ensure compliance with the hiring process and required documents by the HRMO, before JO employees officially report to work; and

Stop assigning to the barangay, JO personnel which will perform work and duties redundant with those of the existing Barangay Officials and employees.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Enjoin the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to identify the
causes of the discrepancies between the PPE Ledger balances and RPCPPE,
and adjust their records accordingly. In addition, create a separate Inventory
Committee for one-time cleansing of PPE required under COA Circular No. 2020-
006 and approve the Physical Inventory Plan necessary to conduct the physical
inventory;
b. Instruct the duly created Inventory Committee, the Municipal General Services
Office and Municipal Accounting Office to adopt the general guidelines, procedural
guidelines, and reconciliation of inventory count per RPCPPE with property and
accounting records required under COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the conduct of
inventory taking for one-time cleansing of PPE;
c. Cause the conduct of an investigation to determine the last known location/office
of non-existing/missing PPE items, the persons accountable therefor, the
circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for such loss. Issue
demand letters to the accountable personnel to produce the properties or pay the
depreciated replacement cost thereof, as provided in Section 7.0 of COA Circular
No. 2020-006;
d. Request authority from the COA for the derecognition in the books of accounts of
those non-existing/missing PPE items for which accountability could not be
established, in accordance with Section 8.0 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
e. Prepare an Inventory and Inspection of Report of Unserviceable Property and all the
necessary documents/reports for the proper disposal of unserviceable assets in
accordance with applicable rules and regulations on disposal procedures; and,
f. Work coordinately with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer on the inventory
of Infrastructure Assets such as Land Improvements, Local Road Network, Flood
Control System, Water Supply System and Other Public Infrastructures.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the proper journal entries for recording of receipts and disbursements under
the Trust Fund. Refrain from making shortcut entries for proper monitoring of the
balances of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the list of unreleased checks every year-end in time
for the closing of the books and submit to the Municipal Accountant;
b. Municipal Accountant to revert back to the Cash in Bank and proper liability
accounts the amount corresponding to the unreleased checks at year-end; and,
c. Municipal Accountant to adjust all valid reconciling items determined during the
preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements such as stale checks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to evaluate
and compare the amount of collectibles and payables with the records of the Provincial
Government to ascertain the legality and validity of both claims so both parties may
make the necessary adjustment on their proper books of accounts to facilitate financial
statements free of any material misrepresentation that could affect the fairness of
presentation of the accounts in the financial statements of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
ensure that the reciprocal accounts Due to Other Fund and Due From Other Fund will
present the same balances at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. In coordination with the Accounting Office and BAC, cause the submission of the
documents required by items by Items 9.1 and 9.1.1 of COA Circular 2012-001 dated
June 14, 2012 to avoid suspension and/or disallowance.
b. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of supporting
documents prescribed under COA Circular No. 2012-001 to ensure compliance with
the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 before processing payments and to establish the
legality, validity and propriety of the disbursements.
c. Through the BAC and the TWG:
- to strictly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its revised IRR;
- ensure that the procedures in the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 and GPPB Manual
of Procedures for the Procurement of Infrastructure Project in the conduct of
post qualification are strictly observed to ensure compliance of the bidder with
Lowest Calculated and Responsive Bid with the eligibility requirements; and,
d. Together with all the municipal officials concerned, to ensure that the projects
undertaken by the Municipality are implemented and completed on time so that the
intended beneficiaries could enjoy the benefits at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Acting MENRO to adopt measures and controls that will
promote reliability of payments made for the garbage disposal at the sanitary
landfill of BSWMI such as monitoring of the trips and the measurement of garbage
before its disposal;
b. Acting MENRO to revisit the provisions enumerated in the MOA for garbage
disposal to include all the amount billed by BSWMI and for its updating regularly;
and,
c. Municipal Accountant to see to it that all required documents are attached and to
submit the lacking documents to substantiate the payments made by the
Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Accountant see to it that all payments for gasoline, fuel and lubricants
shall be supported with driver’s trip tickets properly accomplished and serially
numbered;
b. General Services Officer (GSO) to submit to the Office of the Auditor the Monthly
Report of Official Travels as per COA Circular No. 77-61 at the end of the month;
c. Concerned municipal official to provide explanation on the gasoline consumption
of Foton Ambulance which was reported as unserviceable; and,
d. Acting Municipal Environmental and Natural Resources Officer (MENRO) to submit
explanation or justification why two dump trucks with attached Daily Trip Tickets of
which the information therein were the same except for the plate number.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned Municipal officials to
prioritize the implementation of the project for the provision of a suitable and adequate
size storage facility with installed racks that can accommodate all the financial reports
and other related documents of the Municipality and its 19 barangays considering the
ten-year preservation period of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Engineer to strictly
adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2013-004 to fully inform the public and to
effect full disclosure policy of the transactions governing public interest. Further,
require the concerned municipal officials to submit at the beginning of the year a list
of ongoing and to be implemented PPAs for the year and to inform COA within ten days
after the award of the projects or before the start of the PPAs that the appropriate
projects signboards are already posted on the project sites.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Development
Council, Municipal Planning and Development Office and Budget Office to ensure that
the development projects/activities contained in the Municipality’s Annual Investment
Program shall be budgeted and implemented pursuant to Sections 106 and 305(i) of RA
No. 7160 and Article 410 of its IRR.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk and
Reduction Officer to observe the required format in the preparation of the Monthly
Report on Source and Utilization of LDRRMF and to be certified correct by the
Municipal Accountant and submit on or before the 15th day after the end of each month.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
present the continuing appropriation under the General Fund on the appropriation,
utilization and balances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the
Municipal Accountant to charge only eligible items against the fund following the COA
Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 on the allocation and utilization
of the 5 per cent LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the designated General
Services Officer to:

a. Conduct complete physical count of property, prepare the Report on
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) in the
prescribed format and submit to the Audit Team not later than January 31
of each year;
b. Prepare an Inventory and Inspection of Report of Unserviceable Property
and all the necessary documents/reports for the proper disposal of
unserviceable assets in accordance with applicable rules and regulations on disposal procedures;
c. Work coordinately with the assistance of the Municipal Engineer on the
inventory of Infrastructure Assets such as Land Improvements, Local Road
Network, Flood Control System, Water Supply System and Other Public
Infrastructures; and
d. Coordinate with the Municipal Accountant for the reconciliation of PPE
accounts in order not to compromise the reliability of submitted reports.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal
Accountant to recognize in the books the unrecorded assets arising from Trust
Fund.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal Assessor to facilitate the registration and transfer of the title of the land
properties from the previous owners to the Municipality;
b. Municipal Accountant to submit the disbursement voucher amounting to
₱2,980,000 and the enumerated lacking supporting documents on the land
purchases in CY 2020;
c. Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Land and Land Improvements Ledger
Card (LLILC) with complete description of land; and
d. Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records to come up
with the correct value of land.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal Accountant to provide Allowance for Impairment for the dormant
receivables of ₱181,423.98 for the possible losses;
b. Municipal Accountant to conduct necessary procedures to secure/produce
all relevant and applicable documents under Item 8 of COA Circular No,
2016-005 to support the filing or request for authority to write-off of noted
dormant receivable accounts from the Commission on Audit; and
c. Former Disbursing Officer to produce all the available records pertaining to
her request for relief from accountability for the held up amount of
₱434,903.40 and to follow up on its status.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to
exercise due care in recording, analyzing and classifying Municipality’s financial
transactions to reflect the correct balances of these accounts in order not to
mislead the users of the financial statements.

We commended the Management of the Municipal Government of Lian for the efficient
and prompt implementation of the identified programs, activities and projects towards the
achievement of BGCM’s objective.

However, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:

a. Prepare and submit the subsidiary ledger for the SAGF-BGCM in compliance with
the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for LGUs for fair presentation,
transparency and accountability of the funds utilized;
b. Make the necessary adjusting entries for the receipt and utilization of the BGCM fund
and use the appropriate accounts in recording transactions in compliance with COA
Circular No. 2015-009 and GAM for LGUs;
c. Immediately present for audit the lacking documents to substantiate aforementioned
BGCM transactions and ensure that all documentary requirements for emergency
purchase are complied with before certifying as to completeness of the supporting
documents of the disbursement vouchers; and
d. Submit justification or explanation on the noted deficiency of ₱143,738.57 between
the Report of Utilization and submitted Disbursement Vouchers (DVs).

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to return the amount of ₱21,748.61 to the
BGCM Fund and ensure that subsequent transactions are charged against the
appropriate fund.

For future similar financial assistance, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
instruct the Disbursing Officer in coordination with the Municipal Social Welfare
Development Officer and the barangay officials to assist fully in the compilation of the
documentary requirements in order to efficiently facilitate the immediate liquidation of the
cash advance in consonance with the provision of DSWD MC No. 9, Series of 2020.

Likewise, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Disbursing
Officer and the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer to submit written
justification on the delay in the refund of ₱195,000.00 to DSWD Field Office No. IV-A.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and other
concerned personnel to submit the lacking documents such as: authorization from the
head of agency or office to report during ECQ, Daily Time Record (DTR) and any proof
that the service rendered and accomplishment reports in support for the claims of
Hazard pay and Special Risk Allowance (SRA) in compliance with Administrative Order
No. 26 to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to
submit Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations in support for the year-end
financial statements in conformity with the aforementioned regulation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:

a. MDRRMO to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
LDRRMF with the prescribed format provided in COA Circular 2012-001
certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submit on or before
the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local
Development Council;
b. the MDRRMO to ensure programming of the previous year’s unexpended
balance of the LDRRMF and incorporation such programs and activities
in the LDRRMFIP be taken into consideration;
c. the Municipal Accountant to record the transactions and utilization of
Trust Liability- DRRM in the Trust Fund book and provide complete
disclosure in Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the
provisions of COA Circular 2012-002; and
d. the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the disbursement
vouchers totalling ₱11,703,426.03 paid out of Trust Liability- DRRM to
the audit team for verification and post audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, as head of the Municipal
Development Council, require:

a. the Council to include only projects that are feasible and ready for
implementation with properly planned timing of implementation in its
Annual Investment Program. Ensure optimal utilization of the 20%
Development Fund in the succeeding periods thru thorough review,
reevaluation and reassessment of its planned PPAs; and
b. the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the disbursement
vouchers with supporting documents totaling ₱6,366,369.61 and
strictly observe the provisions of Section 39 of Presidential Decree No.
1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor advise the:

a. BAC Chairman and Technical Working Group (TWG) to properly assess bid
of contractors to avoid overlapping contracts wherein the same key
personnel and equipment is already pledged in other projects;
b. Municipal Engineer to submit computation on the imposed liquidated
damages and documents on the actual percentage of completion on its
targeted date to determine the unperformed work as basis in the
computation of liquidated damages; and
c. Municipal Engineer to monitor the simultaneous implementation projects
by the same contractor to guard against unwarranted delays or
substandard quality of work.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Agency Authorized Officer (AAO) to ensure the sufficiency of the
employee’s net pay after deducting the loan amortizations prior to
approving loans.
b. Municipal Accountant to deduct the correct amount of premiums and
loan amortizations due to avoid incurrence of penalties and remit the
same immediately to GSIS.
c. AAO and Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the GSIS for the
reconciliation of records to determine the correctness of the unpaid
premiums of PS and GS with a total amount of ₱83,666.19 and inform
the concerned municipal employees for the loan accounts which are in
default.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to instruct the:
a. Municipal General Services Officer (GSO) to conduct complete physical count of
property, prepare the Report on Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment
(RPCPPE) in the prescribed format and submit to the Audit Team not later than
January 31 of each year;
b. Municipal GSO and Municipal Accountant to reconcile the difference noted between
the RPCPPE and accounting records in order not to compromise the reliability of
submitted reports;
c. Municipal Accountant to exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPE with lump sum
balances and at the same time secure the necessary documents to reconcile them
with the Inventory Report of GSO;
d. Municipal GSO to prepare an Inventory and Inspection of Report of Unserviceable
Property and all the necessary documents/reports for the proper disposal of
unserviceable assets in accordance with applicable rules and regulations on disposal
procedures; and
e. Municipal GSO, Municipal Engineer and Municipal Accountant to abide with the
provisions of COA Circular No. 2015-008 regarding the accounting and reporting
requirement of the Road Networks.

We again recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the:
a. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile the list of
delinquent real property tax as of year-end against the recorded RPT and SET
Receivables to present an accurate and reliable receivable accounts in the
financial statements; and
b. Municipal Treasurer exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all
delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their dues
on time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the
accuracy of the receivables and its collectibility.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to a) conduct inventory of unreleased and stale
checks on-hand and ensure that each check is properly accounted, b) prepare and
submit to the Municipal Accounting Office the List of Unreleased Checks in time for
the closing of the books to enable the reversion back to cash in bank and payable
accounts of the amount of unreleased checks in order to fairly present the cash in
bank and other affected accounts in the financial statements and c) for the checks
which have become stale, send written notice to the payees for the existence of
checks and require submission to facilitate issuance of new checks; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments of reconciling items
determined during the preparation of the Monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements on
a timely basis for an accurate and reliable Cash in Bank account balance in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
a. the Accounting Department to provide schedule of inventory accounts and
have it reconcile with the records of the GSO. Meanwhile, maintain supplies
ledger cards as prescribed by the rules;
b. the Municipal GSO to conduct the physical count of inventories on a
semestral basis and prepare the RPCI. Submit copies of RPCI to the
Municipal Accountant and to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
Reconcile the results of the count with the accounting records and establish
the causes of discrepancies, whether items had already been missing,
issued, distributed and consumed to arrive at the correct balance in the
Financial Statements. Likewise, always maintain stock cards to properly
monitor and supervise the receipt and issuance of materials and supplies;
and
c. the Human Resource Management Personnel to review the current
organizational structure of the Municipality, specially those offices
mentioned in the audit, conduct a thorough analysis of the workload of each
personnel, and include in the human resource strategic plan possible
provision for additional personnel if found to be deficient.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
hasten the request for write-off/adjustments of the dormant accounts to the
Commission on Audit duly supported with the list of available records pertaining to
the accounts as to the extent of validation made on the accounts, reasons why the
records/documents/schedules/ could not be located and the number of years the
accounts have been dormant.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. See to it that the receivables/payables in the books of the municipality for intra
agency accounts are reconciled which means that all entries under account Due
from/to Other Fund in one particular fund should have a corresponding entry to other
fund; and
b. Coordinate with the Provincial Accountant for account Due from/Due to
LGUs to establish the correct balances in the corresponding subsidiary ledgers and
to ascertain the reliability of both records in order that both the municipality and
province may make the necessary adjustments on their respective books of
accounts.

We recommended that the Local School Board conduct a thorough review on the
Municipality’s resources and its budget. Management should closely monitor/limit its
expenses within the approved appropriations in order to avoid the incurrence of net
loss/deficit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC), the Bid and Award
Committee (BAC) and the Municipal Engineer to ensure the timely, effective
and efficient of the approved priority development projects within their
targeted completion period;
b. Municipal Budget Officer to record obligations based on the actual contract
amount and to coordinate with the MPDC for the implemented projects under
20 per cent DF to ensure that these are duly obligated;
c. Municipal Development Council to (i) conduct in-depth planning for
prioritization of PPAs in the 20 per cent Development Fund by the consensus
of the constituents and coordination with the MPDC to avoid realignment and
sudden change of projects intended for current year’s implementation; and (ii)
ensure that only programs and projects geared towards the attainment of
socio-economic and environmental management outcomes that partake the
nature of investment or capital expenditures, as mandated by the law and its
implementing rules and regulations, will be given allocation under the 20 per
cent DF to ensure full realization of the purpose for which the Fund was
created; and
d. Municipal Budget Officer and MPDC to prepare Supplemental Annual
Investment Plan/Procurement Plan for all procurement of goods/infrastructures
projects under Supplemental Budgets and realignment of fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM) to:
a. Prepare a Local Risk Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) which fully identify and specify the PPAs to be implemented
indicating the place/location and the amount involved to fund each
undertaking.
b. Enforce the full utilization of the programs and projects of the Municipality
under 70% Mitigation Fund, Continuing Appropriation and Trust Liability –
MDRRMF. Have those programs completed within the intended time to avoid
delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
a. GAD Focal person to comply with the provision of Section 4.1 of
PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC 2013-01 and EO No. 273 and coordinate with the
Municipal Budget Officer for the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget ; and
b. Concerned offices to maximize the utilization of GAD Funds and see to it that GAD
related Programs/Projects/Activities will fully addressed and adhere to the basic
purpose and objectives for which the funds were provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Maintain subsidiary ledgers for loan account with DBP based on the approved
Promissory Notes to facilitate reconciliation of balances between the two records so
as to ensure the correctness of this account and in compliance with Section 10 of
NGAS Manual, Volume II; and
b. Determine the actual loan balances by working back on the loan releases/principal
and payments made and if found that the amounts are not the same with the bank
balance coordinate with the DBP officials for the possible reconciliation of the loan
accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Engineer to prepare
and submit the necessary documents enumerated in COA Circular No. 2012-01 for the
timely preparation of report on projects requested for the technical evaluation and
inspection by COA Technical Audit Specialist (TAS).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee
(BAC) to submit the approved Procurement Monitoring Report to the GPPB in printed
and electronic format within the prescribed period in accordance with the provision of the
Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
observe the disclosure requirements in the preparation of the financial statements in
accordance with the PPSASs and Section 3.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-004 to assist
users in understanding the financial statements and to provide them with information
relevant to decision-making.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the financial statements in compliance with the requirements of PPSAS
including the required reconciliation to be disclosed in the Notes to Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the Municipality reconcile employees’ records at GSIS,
PhilHealth and PAG-IBIG to identify the affected employees on the unremitted
balances and immediately remit the amounts due to these agencies and identify the
cause/s of the over remittance for some accounts and make the proper adjustments
to avoid the incurrence of unnecessary financial expenses in the form of imposed
penalties. Likewise, review the taxes withheld to employees, suppliers and
contractors to ensure that all withheld taxes are remitted on the prescribed period to
avoid penalties and surcharges.

observations.managementAction: The GSO is the OIC Budget Officer and still to be perform the duties and responsibilities of her current position. The Municipal Officer responsible for the maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards was set aside. The Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO maintain PPE and Property Ledger Cards, the Accounting Office has been updating the PPE and Property ledger cards while the Treasurer/GSO remained not maintaining the subsidiary ledger and property cards. The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer maintained both PPE and property ledger cards. The donated vehicles from PCSO/donor was registered and transferred in the books of the Municipality but has not been adjusted in the books of accounts. The Municipal Officer responsible for the maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards was set aside. The Municipal Accountant asserted that identification of PPEs included the Depreciation of PPEs were already adjusted and those in the trust fund that need to be transferred were already recognized as PPE's.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower in the accounting dept. since we are only four in the department. The GSO did not have the required personnel for the task. The GSO is also newly designated and maintenance of PPE Ledger Cards was set aside. The data for the inventory is still incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
• Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records with the Municipal Accountant in order to present reliable RPT and SET receivables in the financial statements; and
• Municipal Treasurer exhaust all efforts to collect all those delinquent real property taxes and encourage the taxpayers to pay their dues on time and conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the accuracy of the receivables and its collectibility.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer are reconciling their records regularly however, they commented that unreconciled amount pertained to old balances.
observations.reason: Some unreconciled balances were not totally eliminated. There were cases that property owners cannot be located as all letters were given back to sender.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
• Coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on the receivables/payables and their corresponding balances; and
• Review the composition of the accounts under the Due to LGUs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous recording/inclusion in the Due to LGUs account to ensure that the amounts are valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant and obtained copies of the JEVs showing all the transactions recorded in the books of accounts of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s books of accounts. The Municipal Accountant made the necessary identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper documents before effecting the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: There was an unreconciled amount which pertained to old account for which no reconciled documents can be obtained.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to exhaust efforts to obtain sufficient information on those dormant accounts. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors or accountable officers to notify them of their outstanding balance. Otherwise, if they can no longer be traced, expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit supported with Sangguniang Bayan Resolution requesting the same and certification as to extent of management validation on these accounts, aging schedule and available records pertaining to these dormant accounts, if any.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has taken note of the audit observation and will comply in the ensuing year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the following:
• The Municipal Accountant review the composition of the accounts under the Due to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous recording/inclusion in the Due to NGAs account to ensure that the amounts are valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments; and
• The Municipal Accountant and the Head of MPDC immediately submit the liquidation report and reports if not submitted to the source agency for refund of the unexpended balance. See to it that future fund transfers are fully implemented within the time frame to avoid delay in liquidation; and
• The Head of MPDC coordinate with the concerned department heads to enforce utilization and implementation of those projects that were not yet implemented while pursue the continuation of those ongoing projects and to ensure their respective time frames and schedules are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delays in its implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant immediately submitted the liquidation report and the necessary document to the source agency for refund of the unexpended balance. The Head of the MPDC coordinated with the concerned department heads and the management for implementation of ongoing projects and monitor the schedule of their completion. The Municipal Chief Executive directed concerned department heads to implement the projects and to continue its utilization. MPDC will coordinate with the concerned department and heads to ensure implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to record the adjusting entry recognizing the current portion of the loans payable as Current Liabilities in the financial statement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant recognized the current portion of the loans payable under the Current Liabilities in the statement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned department heads to immediately pursue the settlement of the disallowances in accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts (RRSA). Secure documents such as Notice of Finality of Decision/COA order to trace back those disallowances reflected in the Receivables – Disallowances/Charges account. Once the persons liable and their accountability have been established issue demand letter to notify them of their liability and to enforce collection on them.
observations.reason: This had been in the books for more than 20 years.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) strictly observe the provisions of the IRR of RA 9184 in the prosecution of the Municipality’s infrastructure projects. We further recommended for a written justification from the BAC Chairman for the noted deficiencies and inconsistencies in the procurement process such as: a) non-conduct of pre-procurement conference; b) non- submission of contracts for review and evaluation by the Commission on Audit; c) failure to provide proof of invitation to bid as observers to COA and to a private group in a sector or discipline; d) non-deduction of retention not in accordance with the provisions; and e) non-submission of bid data sheet, instruction to bidders and post-qualification reports.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC commented that the Bids and Awards Committee has been instructed to prepare Procurement Monitoring Report to GPPB. Also, the entire BAC Members had been required to review the individual responsibilities for all matters pertaining to procurement. A refresher training is also being formulated to ensure that all members are on the same page of procurement process. GPBB assistance might be needed for this action so that the BAC can fully comply with all the mandatory actions and documents to ensure a smooth procurement process.
observations.reason: The BAC failed to submit the Procurement Monitoring Reports. GPBB assistance might be needed. No justification for the noted deficiencies was provided.

observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer commented that her Office and the BAC Secretary will initiate effort for the updating of obligations since the Obligation Request was prepared before the bidding. She also added that the preparation of the Supplemental/Annual Investment Plan/Procurement Plan for all procurement of goods/infrastructure project will be strictly observed when there is a realignment of fund and an approved Supplemental Budget. The MPDC asserted that the realignment of the 20% DF totaling ₱16,700,000.00 was done to comply with the directive of the DILG Circular No. 2019-121 dated July 29, 2019 which mandates the LGUs to clear roads of illegal structures and constructions. Some barangay halls were demolished to comply with that circular. He also added that the realigned amount was used to purchase lots that are intended to serve as a new location for the demolished barangay halls, while some of the funds was also used for road concreting projects.
observations.reason: The Audit Team opined that construction of barangay hall is not among the allowable development projects chargeable against the 20% DF under DILG and DBM JMC No. 2018-1 dated February 22, 2017. Thorough planning should be conducted to minimize realignment of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the GAD Focal Person to comply with the provision of Section 4.1 of PCW/DILG/DBM/NEDA JMC 2013-01 and EO No. 273 and coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer for the preparation of GAD Plan and Budget; and Concerned offices to maximize the utilization of GAD Funds and see to it that GAD related Programs/Projects/Activities will fully addressed and adhere to the basic purpose and objectives for which the funds were provided.
observations.managementAction: No reply was received from Management and the newly designated GAD Focal Person for the AOM issued by the Audit Team.
observations.reason: The newly designated GAD Focal Person is not yet aware on the rules and regulations regarding GAD appropriation, utilization and reporting.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management Officer to make a study for possible creation of a regular plantilla position for an additional staff who will assist the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of detailed engineering and monitoring of project implementation.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal management has not yet created regular position for additional staff to assist the Municipal Engineer in the conduct of detailed engineering and monitoring of project implementation; however a study will be undertaken.
observations.reason: Personal limitation will be an issue for this matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) and the Technical Working Group (TWG) to submit justification on the failure of the members of the BAC and TWG to conduct proper and correct procedures in the bid evaluation and post-qualification processes and to check if the Municipality’s on-going projects with claims of contractors with delayed completion have negative slippage of at least 15% in any one project or a negative slippage of at least 10% in each of 2 or more contracts in order to disqualify the contractor in the award; scrutinize the contractor’s sufficiency for manpower and equipment on all projects to be awarded and not on individual project only in cases where the contractor is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or overlapping implementation period; and conduct the required processes and procedures in all the stages of the bidding in the procurement of infrastructure and strictly comply with all the documentary requirements mandated by RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: There are still cases that the processes and procedures required were not observed. The BAC and Secretariat were required to attend procurement training and update their knowledge on procurement processes.
observations.reason: Documents were not submitted to the municipal officials. Some procedures were not conducted and explained. No justification was submitted to the new Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to provide the explanation for the inconsistencies in the procurement processes and to submit all the documents to the Auditor concerned to check the propriety of the award of contracts and for further evaluation of the transaction to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit; and
• Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of required supporting documents before approval of the claim.
observations.managementAction: The MPDC commented that the Bids and Awards Committee has been instructed to prepare Procurement Monitoring Report to GPPB. The Municipal Accountant will check the completeness of supporting documents before processing the claims.
observations.reason: Some required documents were not submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned officials and employees to stop deducting personal loans contracted by the employees in their personal capacity and stop acting as collecting and remitting agent of unauthorized institution. Likewise, strictly comply with the GAA provision that monthly take home pay of employee shall not be lower than Four Thousand Pesos (₱4,000.00).
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will strictly comply with the cited law, rules and regulations and pursue the necessary monitoring to avoid further deductions that may lower the monthly take home pay.
observations.reason: Some employees’ net take home pay are below ₱4,000.00.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor immediately undertake the material recovery facility and its equipment and make it operational immediately to help address the solid waste management problem of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Materials Recovery Facility and its equipment are now operational.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor organize the Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC) and activate, strengthen, and sustain it pursuant to Section 15 of RA 9344.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Council for the Protection of Children (MCPC) was not yet organized. The municipal officials will issue an administrative order to organize it.
observations.reason: No action was taken. This matter was not prioritized by Management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the department head/s or personnel concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for CY 2017 and on every AAR thereafter.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor submitted the Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) on August 24, 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Sections 321 and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and its Implementing Rules and Regulations and avoid any practices that undermines the integration of planning and budgeting at the local level.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality made some realignments to accommodate essential expenses affecting the municipality operation. The Municipal Budget Officer and the MPDC will ensure strict compliance of the rules and regulations regarding realignment of funds.
observations.reason: Realignments were made due to circumstances and some were necessary. Some were not in accordance with the Act but were done to address emergencies and operational needs.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our findings that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Inventory and Appraisal Committee to conduct the LGU’s complete inventory taking of all municipal properties, prepare the RPCPPE and submit to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor not later than January 31 of each year; • The General Services Officer to prepare and maintain complete Property Cards and update the PPE ledger cards while the Municipal Accountant update the accounting records; and • Cause the titling of the parcels of land in favor of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Conducted partial inventory of the LGU’s properties during the spare time. Newly designated GSO has started to prepare the necessary inventory records and the Municipal Accountant has started the updating of the ledger cards. The donated motor vehicle was registered and recorded in the Municipality but not yet adjusted in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The incumbent GSO was only recently appointed and is still preparing the inventory. The donated motor vehicle was recorded but not yet adjusted due to missing documents. Titling of parcels of land has not been completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to record with the Accounting Office the correct amounts and reconcile the records. Strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize delinquencies and request assistance from Sanggunian for enforcement of remedies for collection.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer reconciled her reports with the Municipal Accountant and strengthened the collection efforts by intensifying collection campaigns.
observations.reason: No Sanggunian resolution regarding request for assistance in enforcement was made. Some discrepancies were addressed but old balances remain.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant coordinate with the Provincial Accountant and the concerned offices and reconcile the balances and present the JEVs for the necessary adjustments.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant coordinated with the Provincial Accountant and obtained copies of the JEVs showing all transactions recorded in the books of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s books of account.
observations.reason: Unreconciled amounts pertain to old balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer to open a special depository account for the EUF for easy monitoring and compliance with Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs; maintain and provide complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the operation of the Tourism Office; and the Municipal Administrator to organize a committee that will oversee the proper implementation/utilization of the Educational Fund Program to ensure that only qualified/deserving students will be benefited.
observations.managementAction: A subsidiary ledger for the trust fund was maintained. The Municipal released educational assistance of ₱5,000.00 to an enrolled first year college student. An ordinance for the Tourism Office has been proposed and will be enacted.
observations.reason: No clear policy was provided and some funds were erroneously credited; the Tourism Office operational policy not yet finalized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Heads of different Offices in coordination with MPDC as the lead man to plan for the immediate action/turned over to the concerned barangay/ targeted recipients; ensure that Project completed is turned over to the proper barangay/target recipients and provide report of action taken on the aforementioned unutilized projects to avoid wastage of government funds.
observations.managementAction: Some of the completed projects were already turned-over to the concerned barangays and recipients. The MPDC will coordinate with concerned offices to ensure completed projects are turned over. Action has been initiated to monitor utilization and usage of the projects.
observations.reason: Some projects were not yet turned over and reports were not submitted; lack of coordination with barangays and recipients delayed turnover.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Management to create additional plantilla position for an additional staff to assist the Municipal Engineer and ensure strict monitoring of project implementation and timely imposition of liquidated damages where necessary.
observations.managementAction: The municipality initiated actions to create additional plantilla positions and the Municipal Engineer to monitor implementation. Some additional personnel were requested.
observations.reason: Personal limitation and plantilla creation pending; some extensions were necessary due to project conditions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to submit justification on the failure of BAC/TWG members to conduct proper evaluation; check contractors claims for slippage and disqualify where appropriate; scrutinize contractor’s sufficiency for manpower and equipment; and strictly comply with documentary requirements of RA 9184.
observations.managementAction: There are still lapses in the procurement processes; BAC members were instructed to attend procurement training; Municipal officials to improve procedures.
observations.reason: Lacking documents and processes; no full compliance yet; required trainings and documentation pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC to provide explanations for procurement inconsistencies and submit documents to the Auditor; Municipal Accountant to check completeness of supporting documents before approval of claims.
observations.managementAction: The BAC was required to provide explanations and submit documentation; Municipal Accountant to check completeness of supporting documents.
observations.reason: Some missing documents were not submitted to the Auditor; explanations not fully provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC, Secretariat and TWG to provide explanation on the inconsistencies and to attend procurement training to update and carry-out procurement tasks efficiently and effectively.
observations.managementAction: The BAC, Secretariat and TWG were requested to provide updates and undertake procurement training; meetings were conducted to address the issues.
observations.reason: Some explanations were provided but documents remained incomplete; trainings and full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipality stop the practice of deducting personal loans and stop acting as collecting and remitting agent of unauthorized institutions and strictly comply with GAA provision that monthly take home pay shall not be lower than ₱4,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant will comply with the observation and will ensure compliance in the ensuing year.
observations.reason: Some employees’ salaries still below ₱4,000.00; monitoring ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor provide a building for safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents generated by the various departments and provide storeroom for submission of vouchers and documents for preservation.
observations.managementAction: Some office re-arrangements were made and proposals for a building were prepared; no final action completed.
observations.reason: No available fund to construct dedicated building; initiative underway.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant immediately submit the missing disbursement vouchers and its related supporting documents to the Auditor and strictly comply with the cited law, rules and regulations relative to the timely submission of accounts to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: Partially complied; some disbursement vouchers were submitted.
observations.reason: Some DVs remained unsubmitted resulting to partial implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the establishment of GAD Database; ensure timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and DILG; ensure GAD mainstreaming and that GAD Plan and Accomplishment Report are prepared and submitted as required.
observations.managementAction: Some GAD activities were implemented and funds disbursed; GAD reporting to be improved and new GAD Focal Person to ensure compliance.
observations.reason: Focal Point Person to submit required reports; some activities not fully linked to gender concerns.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review in detail all entries made on the Due from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Trust Fund and check any unrecorded transaction/s in its reciprocal accounts. Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will adjust the accounts and effect the necessary JEVs to reconcile the balances.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant has not yet adjusted the accounts; some reconciling items pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Management to conduct a feasibility study for the operation of the municipality’s slaughterhouse and ensure safety and quality of meat products being sold in the market.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Management conducted a partial feasibility study and is assessing relocation and re-organization of the slaughterhouse and market operations.
observations.reason: No final decision made and additional funds required for full rehabilitation and relocation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor perform a thorough study and evaluation on the actual workloads and manpower requirements for each department/office to avoid unnecessary jobs and minimize government spending.
observations.managementAction: A study will be undertaken to assess workload and manpower requirements; recruitment freeze and rationalization will be considered.
observations.reason: No complete study yet; workloads still being assessed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that concerned officials and employees submit the required documents immediately for validation and ensure compliance to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension and eventual Notice of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant required submission of supporting documents and some were submitted; others remain incomplete.
observations.reason: Not all supporting documents were provided; some claims still pending validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials and employees to refund the PEI received in excess of the ₱5,000.00 limit set under DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3.
observations.managementAction: The matter was pursued and some refunds initiated; awaiting decision on appeal for remaining disallowances.
observations.reason: Awaiting decision on appeal made by the Municipality; some refunds were made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the officials and employees to refund the excess PEI as per DBM Circular and to comply with the regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality filed an appeal and is awaiting the COA decision.
observations.reason: Awaiting COA decision on the appeal.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to request from the Provincial Accountant the copies of the Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs) showing all transactions recorded in the books of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant will visit the Provincial Accounting Office to reconcile their recorded receivables and payables. The Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of entries and make necessary adjustments.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant has not yet coordinated with the Provincial Accounting Office for reconciliation to establish the correct balances and secure the JEVs for validation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: • Municipal Treasurer to open a special depository account for the Environmental User’s Fee (EUF) for easy monitoring of collection and compliance with Ordinance No. 07 of 2016; • Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 313 of RA 7160 and Section 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs and maintain complete subsidiary ledgers for Tourism Office financial statements; and • Municipal Administrator to organize a committee to oversee proper implementation/utilization of the Educational Fund Program to ensure only qualified students benefit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has been requested to decide on the recommended course of action and open the special account. The Municipal Accountant to review the accounting classification and maintain subsidiary ledgers. The Municipal Administrator to organize a committee for policy-making and oversight.
observations.reason: The Municipality has not yet opened the special depository account for EUF and has not yet fully decided on the course of action. The Educational Fund implementation policy and oversight committee have not been fully organized.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC and TWG to: • submit justification for noted deficiencies in post-qualification and bid evaluation; • check if any contractor has negative slippage affecting capacity and disqualify if warranted; • scrutinize contractor’s sufficiency of manpower and equipment across all awarded projects; and • strictly comply with all documentary requirements prescribed by RA 9184 in all procurement stages.
observations.managementAction: The BAC performed additional reviews and conducted scrutiny of contractors' capabilities. Justifications for evaluation procedures were provided and TWG retrained on post-qualification requirements.
observations.reason: Documentation for post-qualification stages were not consistently maintained for all projects and some deficiencies in evaluation procedures were observed. Formal post-qualification reports were not always prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require BAC, BAC Secretariat and TWG members to provide explanation on the inconsistencies and require new members to undergo training or capacity development to update them on procurement responsibilities.
observations.managementAction: BAC, Secretariat and TWG provided explanations and planned capacity building sessions for members and secretariat to improve procurement handling.
observations.reason: Although explanations and plans for training were provided, the training and full documentation of corrective actions were not yet completed.

observations.recommendation:
Based on the foregoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor
require the following:
• The Inventory Committee headed by the General Services Officer prepare inventory
list by category and perform the actual and complete physical count of PPE in order
to determine the existence of the recorded PPE accounts and reconcile the results
of the count with the accounting records and establish the causes of discrepancies,
whether items have already been missing, donated, sold and unserviceable to arrive
at the correct balance in the financial statements. As to the infrastructure assets,
coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to verify their existence, prepare and
maintain the required Property Cards and update ARE to facilitate the reconciliation
of RPCPPE with the accounting records;
• The Accounting Department exhaust all efforts to fully identify the PPEs included in
the beginning/unidentified balances. Secure the necessary documents to support
those PPEs to be able to reconcile with the Inventory Report of the GSO. Also, trace
those PPEs without depreciation and with negative balances to establish their correct
carrying value. Likewise, review the composition of the accounts under those PPEs
and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit,
including those PPEs disbursed in the Trust Fund. Secure proper documentation
before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous recording or for
inclusion to ensure that the amounts are valid and conform to the rules and
regulations that will cover such adjustments; and
• The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer maintain complete and
updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the
reconciliation of both records.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
• Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their records to
present a reliable RPT and SET Receivables in the financial statements;
• Municipal Treasurer to exhaust all efforts and devise strategies to collect all
delinquent real property taxes and encourage the tax payer to pay their dues on
time. Conduct verification on those outstanding balances to determine the
accuracy of the receivables and its collectability and
• Municipal Treasurer to submit copy of the document providing the legal basis for
the 0.5% assessment rate of the real property of Absolut Chemicals Inc. in
Barangay Malaruhatan that was used in the computation of levied special
education tax.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and her
staff to:
• Coordinate again with the Provincial Accountant and reconcile their records on
receivables/payables and their corresponding payables/receivables in the books
of the Province to establish the correct balances of the intra-agency accounts;
• Coordinate with the concerned officials and departments heads to obtain
information on those long outstanding accounts. Secure the necessary
documents to properly identify the creditor/local government unit to whom it’s
due. Once the specific local government unit had been identified, verify the
reported balances in the subsidiary ledgers through confirmation letters and
exhaust efforts to fully ascertain the validity and legality of those obligations; and
• Review the composition of the accounts under the Due to LGUs and identify the
appropriate classification for those items identified in the audit. Secure proper
documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting entries for erroneous
recording/inclusion in the Due to LGUs account to ensure that the amounts are
valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover such adjustments.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and the
Municipal Treasurer to exhaust efforts to obtain sufficient information on those
dormant accounts. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation
of the existence of the receivables and determine the concerned debtors or
accountable officers to notify them of their outstanding balance. Otherwise, if they
can no longer be traced, expedite the submission of the request for write-off of these
dormant accounts to the Commission on Audit supported with Sangguniang Bayan
Resolution requesting the same and certification as to extent of management
validation on these accounts, aging schedule and available records pertaining to
these dormant accounts, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the following:
• The Municipal Accountant to review the composition of the accounts under the Due
to NGAs and identify the appropriate classification for those items identified in the
audit. Secure proper documentation before effecting the necessary adjusting
entries for erroneous recording/inclusion in the Due to NGAS account to ensure
that the amounts are valid and conform to the rules and regulations that will cover
such adjustments;
• The Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the liquidation reports if not yet
submitted and the check to the source agency for refund of the unexpended
balance. See to it that future fund transfers are fully implemented within the time
frame to avoid delay in liquidation; and
• The Head of the Municipal Planning and Development Office coordinate with the
concerned department heads to enforce the utilization and implementation of
those projects that were not yet implemented while pursue the continuation of
those ongoing utilization. Likewise, see to it that their respective time frames and
schedules are strictly monitored and evaluated to avoid delays in its
implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to
record the adjusting entry recognizing the current liability portion of the loans payable
and reducing the long term obligations to the proper amount to fairly present the
accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned department heads
