to immediately initiate action to enforce the settlement of the disallowances in
accordance with the COA Revised Rules of Procedure on the Settlement of Accounts
(RRSA). Secure documents such as the Notice of Finality of Decision/COA order of
Execution to trace back those disallowances reflected in the Receivables –
Disallowances/Charges account. Once the persons liable and their accountability
have been established issue demand letter to notify them of their liability and to
enforce collection on them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee
to strictly observe the provisions of the RIRR of RA 9184 in the prosecution of the
Municipality’s infrastructure projects.

We further recommended for a written justification from the BAC Chairman for the noted
deficiencies and inconsistencies in the procurement process such as: a) non-conduct of
pre-procurement conference; b) non-submission of contracts for review and evaluation
by the Commission on Audit; c) failure to provide proof of invitation to bid as observers
to COA and to a private group in a sector or discipline; d) non-deduction of retention not
in accordance with the provisions and e) non-submission of bid data sheet, instruction to
bidders and post-qualification reports.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Engineer and the Municipal
Planning and Development Officer ensure effective utilization of the 20%
Development Fund which is primarily intended to fund projects that shall contribute
to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and environmental
management outcomes.

We further recommended that the development projects/activities contained in the
Municipality’s Annual Investment Program shall be the priority for budgeting and
implementation pursuant to Sections 106 and 305(i) of RA No. 7160 and Article 410 of
Act Implementing Rules and Regulations.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly comply with the aforementioned
provisions of Republic Act No. 9710 or “The Magna Carta of Women” and Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2016-01 and 2013-01 of the Philippine Commission on
Women, Department of the Interior and Local Government, Department of Budget and
Management and National Economic and Development Authority; that the LGU shall
allocate at least five (5) percent of its total budget appropriations for gender and
development; shall establish and maintain an operational GAD database which is one
of the institutional mechanisms for gender mainstreaming; shall implement DILG-
endorsed original or adjusted GAD plan and budget and dutifully prepare GAD Plan
and Budget and Accomplishment Report according to the prescribed format.

Based on the forgoing observations, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
• Require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (MDRRM)
to enforce the implementation of the programs and projects of the Municipality
under 70% Mitigation Fund and have those programs completed within the
intended time to avoid delay in the delivery of services and benefits to the
constituents;
• Require LDRRM Officer to submit the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF
on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through LDRRMC and Local
Development Council (LDC) to the COA Auditor pursuant to COA Circular 2012-
002. Also, coordinate with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal
Accountant to prepare the report in accordance with the prescribed format and
have the amounts reconciled with their records; and
• Require the Municipal Accountant to disclose in the Notes to the Financial
Statements the amount and details of the unexpended balance of LDRRMF as well
as the other required disclosure in order to inform the users of relevant
information.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly comply with the provisions of
Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017 of the Department of Education, Department of
Budget and Management and Department of the Interior and Local Government in
order to ensure that Special Education Fund (SEF) will be utilized for the intended
purpose or according to the identified priority areas listed in the Annual
Implementation Plan (AIP) culled from the DepEd-approved School Improvement
Plan (SIP).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the disbursement vouchers
and supporting documents to the Auditor within the prescribed period to avoid
suspensions/disallowances in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant in
coordination with Municipal Human Resource Management Officer to regularly
reconcile the balances in accounting records with the records of the GSIS in order to
ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the amount Due to GSIS in the financial
statements.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly implement the Safe Closure and
Rehabilitation Plan (SCRP) with the detailed plan and corresponding cost estimate
that was duly acknowledged by the Environment and Management Bureau (EMB),
CALABARZON Region to insure that the controlled dumpsite closure and
rehabilitation are done in a proper manner, with environmental protection measures
and safeguards in place.

We further recommended to put the material recovery facility and its equipment
operational immediately to help address the solid waste management problem of the
Municipality or to help improve its solid waste management program.

We recommended that the LGU organize the Municipal Council for the Protection of
Children (MCPC) and activate, strengthen, and sustain it pursuant to Section 15 of
Republic Act No. 9344 or the “Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the department head/s or personnel
concerned to comply with the completion and submission to the Audit Team of the
Agency Action Plan and Status of Implementation (AAPSI) for part III of CY 2018 AAR
and on every AAR thereafter.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe the provisions of Sections 321
and 336 of Republic Act No. 7160 and Article 417 454(b)of the Act Implementing
Rules and Regulations and promote the Code objectives of discouraging careless
preparation of the annual budget or any practice that undermines the integration of
planning and budgeting at the local level.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Inventory and Appraisal Committee conduct LGU’s inventory and prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the Municipal Accountant and Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of every year; • The General Services Officer, after the preparation of said RPCPPE, submit to the Municipal Accountant the inventory listing for reconciliation; • The Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively; and • Cause the titling of the parcel of land in favor of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Conducted partial inventory of the LGU’s properties data for the undertaking There was an ongoing reconciliation between the said GSO and Accounting records. There was an ongoing effort for the disposal of all unserviceable properties; The donated motor vehicle was registered and transferred Implemented The Municipal Accountant and the Accounting Office has been updating the ledger. The process of titling is ongoing.
observations.reason: The incumbent GSO was only recently appointed and their office has limited manpower. The data for the reconciliation between the RPCPPE and the accounting records were incomplete. The donated vehicle was registered and transferred to the Municipality but has not yet been adjusted in the books. Titling is ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the: • Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning of the year with a certified list and the amount due and collectible; • Municipal Treasurer to reconcile their certified list of taxpayers with the Municipal Assessor’s assessment reports; and • The Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan adopt measures to address delinquent taxpayers, strengthen and intensify collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer provided the taxpayers list and the amount of the tax due and collectible. The Municipal Accountant to reconcile their certified list with the Municipal Assessor’s reports. The Municipal Mayor and the Sanggunian Bayan adopted actions to intensify collection and enforcement measures.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant provided the certified list and reconciliations and the Municipal Treasurer provided taxpayers list; however, reconciliation between the set-up receivables in General Fund and Special Education Fund showed differences related to assessment rate used for certain properties (Absolut Chemicals Inc.) and documents supporting adjustment were not yet submitted. Collection efforts are ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer-OIC, Budget Officer and MPDC to: • prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in accordance with COA Circular No. 2012-002; • re-appropriate the continuing appropriation particularly the procurement of equipment; and • cancel the obligation for the aborted purchase of rubber boat and include the balance in the continuing appropriations of LDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepared the LDRRMFIP and included projects from prior years. The continuing appropriation has been reappropriated. The Municipal Engineer and MPDC coordinated on planned evacuation center facilities.
observations.reason: Some items were provided and basic facilities for evacuation center were committed in CY2018 but other items remained to be provided and projects carried over. The cancelled obligation and reappropriation actions were undertaken but some adjustments remained pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to: • ensure that the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan will be fully implemented; • coordinate with Bauan Solid Waste Management, Inc. (BSWMI) for proper disposal arrangements and lessen dumping charges; • see to it that the bio-composting facility donated will be made operational and that the required equipment will be operational; • coordinate with the BAC to expedite procurement of plastic shredder and densifier if necessary; • enforce eco-bag usage and deputize barangay enforcement officers; and • submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Solid Waste Management Board administered the approved Solid Waste Management Plan and the Municipal Budget Officer ensured that only committed and awarded contracts were obligated in the registry. The MENRO coordinated with BSWMI and prepared actions for composting facility and procurement of needed equipment.
observations.reason: While the dumpsite was closed and a contract with BSWMI established for residual disposal, many closure plan items (planting vegetation, billboards, RCA, full operation of MRF) were not implemented due to manpower constraints, equipment issues and delayed procurements. Documentation requested by the audit team was not yet fully submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Heads of different Offices in coordination with MPDC to: • plan immediate actions/measures to address delay in the actual operation and usage of the projects; • enhance policies and procedures to ensure funds are effectively utilized; • ensure projects are properly coordinated with target recipients and equip them with knowledge and skills; and • provide the Audit Team with report of action taken for the unutilized projects to avoid wastage and possible audit disallowance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal offices coordinated with MPDC and plan implementation and monitoring activities. Actions to equip recipient barangays with skills and knowledge were initiated and some projects were coordinated for turnover.
observations.reason: Although planning and coordination efforts were initiated, several projects remain not yet operational and reports on actions taken had not been fully submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: • Municipal Engineer to use PERT/CPM in estimating required working days, conduct strict compliance to PERT/CPM and submit Statement of Work Accomplished for unperformed portions signed by the Municipal Engineer and contractor; • Human Resource Management Officer to study creation of plantilla positions in the Office of the Municipal Engineer for additional personnel; and • MPDC/BAC Chairman to ensure TWG/BAC undertake proper procurement planning to avoid delays and assist municipal engineer in monitoring.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer observed the prescribed PERT/CPM tool and conducted detailed engineering and monitoring activities. The MPDC and BAC conducted meetings to improve procurement process oversight. Study for additional plantilla undertaken.
observations.reason: The Municipal Engineer implemented PERT/CPM and improved monitoring; however, some contract extension documentary requirements and validation of performance securities remained to be strengthened. Creation of additional plantilla was under study.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require BAC Chairman to provide explanation for inconsistencies in documentation across procurement phases and submit all lacking documents to the Auditor to verify propriety of award and disbursements; and Municipal Accountant to check completeness of required documents before approval of claims.
observations.managementAction: The BAC consolidated documentation for tourism projects and explained noted inconsistencies. The Municipal Accountant strengthened checklist review prior to approval of claims.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents remained incomplete and not all explanations were submitted to the Auditor. Improvements in document management and procurement checklists were underway.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require concerned officials to stop unauthorized deductions, ensure monthly take-home pay of employees is not below ₱4,000 and comply with GAA limits.
observations.managementAction: The concerned offices were directed to stop the practice of deducting personal loans and acting as collecting/remitting agents. Payroll checks were reviewed to ensure compliance with minimum take-home pay.
observations.reason: Some employees justified loans used for family education expenditures. The practice was being corrected and monitoring instituted, but full compliance and remediation were still in progress.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile with GSIS records, refund disallowed government share if required, stop the practice and revert amount to Unappropriated Surplus if no government share is required and to designate an electronic Remittance File (eRF) handler for GSIS eBilling and Collection System.
observations.managementAction: The municipal office initiated refund process and reconciliation with GSIS. Appointment of new liaison officer for GSIS matters was undertaken.
observations.reason: Some refunds were posted and reconciliation with GSIS initiated but full settlement of differences and adjustments awaited GSIS confirmation and issuance of executory disallowance, if any.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal government to prioritize establishment or provision of office space or storeroom for safekeeping of documents and ensure timely submission of documents to the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality identified options for office space and started planning for document preservation. The Municipal Budget Officer ensured committed contracts were recorded in registry.
observations.reason: The Municipality had started steps towards securing documents storage but construction or acquisition of a dedicated building had not been completed. Some documents remained to be organized for submission.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the missing disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and strictly comply with timely submission of accounts to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant initiated review and submission of missing disbursement vouchers and committed to comply with timely submission.
observations.reason: Some vouchers were submitted while others remained to be located and provided; reconciliation and submission continued.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the LGU to: a) establish GAD database; b) ensure timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget to PPDO and DILG; and c) mainstream gender in local development planning and ensure expenditures are aligned to GAD priorities and reported in RAAO.
observations.managementAction: The GAD focal persons initiated steps to collect data and prepare GAD database; submissions to PPDO and DILG were planned and some GAD activities were implemented.
observations.reason: While some GAD activities were funded and some data collection initiated, a complete operational GAD database, DILG-endorsed GAD Plan and Budget, and fully aligned expenditures were not yet in place.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review in detail all entries made on the Due from/to Other Funds to check any unrecorded transactions, and draw the Journal Entry Voucher to make necessary adjustments to present correct balances.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant reviewed entries and prepared adjustments where necessary; coordination with concerned offices was undertaken.
observations.reason: Some adjustments were made but full reconciliation with all counterpart accounts required further information gathering and confirmation from other funds' records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a) creation of a team to study improvement of market operations and revenue collection; b) accept new lessees to fill vacancies; c) intensify collection of delinquent rentals and monitor compliance; d) enjoin Business Permit and Licensing Office and BPLO to monitor business permits and collection; and e) conduct feasibility study for operation and possible relocation/improvement of slaughterhouse.
observations.managementAction: A formal inspection team was created to examine market operations. New lessees were posted to fill vacancies. Business Office and BPLO stepped up monitoring and tax mapping. A feasibility study for slaughterhouse/location was conducted.
observations.reason: Some actions such as vacancy posting and intensified collection were implemented; feasibility study and full operational improvements awaited completion and additional actions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Municipal Mayor require BAC members to be on 'jury duty' type assignment from opening of bids until Notice of Award to complete procurement process; and to invite other possible suppliers to avoid monopoly.
observations.managementAction: BAC observed the required period in subsequent procurements and broadened invitation to other suppliers.
observations.reason: BAC adjusted its procedures to ensure continuity of BAC membership through the procurement stages and widened supplier invitations to enhance competition.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Human Resource Office to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on actual workloads and manpower requirements to avoid unnecessary jobs and minimize spending.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality commenced workload studies and evaluations of departmental manpower requirements and began regularizing recruitment where justified.
observations.reason: Some workload analyses were conducted and steps initiated to rationalize JO positions, but comprehensive review across all departments remained ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require Accounting Office to stop processing payments with incomplete supporting documents and ensure concerned officials submit immediately required documents for validation to avoid issuance of Notice of Suspension/Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Accounting Office strengthened documentary checklist and stopped processing claims with incomplete supporting documents. Concerned officials were advised to submit lacking documents.
observations.reason: Some supporting documents remained incomplete; compliance improved but full documentation and validation for all prior claims were not yet completed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require GSO and BAC to consolidate requests for procurement of supplies and materials of all offices/departments and ensure procurement is done through public biddings instead of shopping.
observations.managementAction: GSO and BAC consolidated procurement requirements and adjusted procurement planning to favor public bidding where practicable.
observations.reason: The municipality observed procurement planning and competitive procurement for subsequent procurements, reducing reliance on shopping.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require officials and employees to refund PEI amounts in excess of the ₱5,000 limit set under DBM Budget Circular and take corrective action to prevent recurrence.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality initiated refund of disallowed PEI amounts and implemented controls to ensure PEI awards comply with DBM limits.
observations.reason: The Municipality started refunding disallowed amounts and adjusted PEI awarding procedures in compliance with DBM circulars; the disallowance execution was pending final COA order.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require officials and employees to refund excess PEI amounts per DBM circular and implement controls for future compliance.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality complied with COA Order upon issuance and arranged refunds where necessary.
observations.reason: The Municipality complied once the disallowance became final and executory and arranged refunds for the excess PEI amounts.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review in detail all entries made on the Due from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Trust Fund and check any unrecorded transaction/s in its reciprocal account/s. Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant said that she was not able to make adjustment or correction because she was not able to trace the Due from Other Funds account of ₱60,204.57.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant was not able to trace the Due from Other Funds ₱60,204.57.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor require officials and employees to refund the PEI received in excess of the ₱5,000.00 limit set under DBM Budget Circular No. 2014-3.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government is waiting for the decision of COA on the appeal filed for the PEI issue on CY 2012. No further action until COA decision.
observations.reason: The management is waiting for COA decision on the appeal filed.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor require officials and employees to refund the PEI received in excess of the ₱5,000.00 limit set under DBM Budget Circular No. 2013-3.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Government is waiting for the decision of COA on the appeal filed for the PEI issue on CY 2012.
observations.reason: Waiting for COA decision on appeal.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to retrieve records to substantiate the claims and to send a follow-up letter for the request for write-off or submit updated request for write-off supported with Municipality’s legal authority and list of available records pertaining to the accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had requested twice to the SB for authority to write-off but was rejected by previous Auditor for lack of supporting documents. The Municipal Accountant requested records to substantiate accounts without success.
observations.reason: Old accounts already existing in the books and lacking supporting documents; attempts to secure records were unsuccessful.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to unearth details regarding outstanding Disallowances/Charges and make documented representations with this Commission requesting assistance for provision of appropriate documents, or to take up with concerned parties for settlement.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant had already exerted representation to unearth the documents but to no avail.
observations.reason: Could not retrieve supporting documents; details as to when the disallowance occurred were incomplete.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor require the:
•••• Inventory and Appraisal Committee conduct complete inventory taking of all
the municipal properties, prepare the Report on the Physical Count of
Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) and submit to the Municipal
Accountant and Auditor concerned not later than January 31 of every year;
•••• The General Services Officer, after the preparation of said RPCPPE,
reconcile with that of the Accounting records and exert effort for the
disposal of all unserviceable properties; obtain documents of the donated
vehicles from PCSO/donor agency, for immediate recording by the
Municipal Accountant in the PPE accounts;
•••• The Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO maintain complete and
updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate
the reconciliation of both records; and
•••• Cause the titling of the parcel of land in favor of the Municipality.

We recommended that:
•••• The Municipal Accountant be careful in the recording/setting-up of
RPT/SET Receivable, make the necessary adjusting entries for the erroneous
set-up of RPT/SET Receivable and then reconcile with the Municipal
Treasurer’s Report of Delinquency;
•••• The Municipal Treasurer provide the Municipal Accountant at the beginning
of the year with a certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the
amount due and collectible for the year; reconcile their certified list of
taxpayers with the Municipal Assessors assessment roll/reports; and copy
furnish the Sangguniang Bayan with the Report of Delinquency together
with the statement of the reason or reasons for such non-collection or non-
payment for its appropriate action; and
•••• The Municipal Mayor, as well as the Sanggunian Bayan Members, adopt
measures to collect from delinquent taxpayers. Strengthen and intensify
collection system and tax campaign to minimize tax delinquencies.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the municipal
records with the Provincial Accountant and settle discrepancies between the
balances of reciprocal accounts so that the Municipality will be able to present the
correct balances of inter-agency receivables and payables in the financial
statements.
Likewise, that the Municipal Accountant request from the Provincial
Accountant the copies of the JEVs showing all the transactions recorded in the
books of accounts of the Province that should be taken up in the Municipality’s
books of accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Disaster
Risk Reduction and Management Officer -OIC, Budget Officer and the
MPDC, to:
• prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund
Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in accordance with the prescribed format of
COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, include in the LDRRMFIP under a
separate caption the projects and activities to be charged to unexpended
LDRRMF of previous years.
• re-appropriate the continuing appropriation particularly the budget for the
procurement of Office Equipment of P67,200.00 and IT Equipment of
P77,000.00 and also the savings/balances of implemented projects that
remained in the continuing appropriations of the capital outlay of
LDRRMF. Moreover, the Municipal Budget Officer make a cancellation on
the obligation made on the aborted Purchase of Rubber Boat with Engine
amounting to P425,000.00 and include in the balance of continuing
appropriations of LDRRMF.
• the Municipal Engineer, MPDC, MDRRMO and Municipal Budget Officer
work together in order to ensure that the evacuation center will be provided
with basic facilities that will protect and comfort the evacuees in times of
calamity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MENRO to:
• See to it that the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan of Municipality’s
Dumpsite will be fully implemented;
• Coordinate with the Management of Bauan Solid Waste Management, Inc.
(BSWMI) for possible utilization of accreditation fee for October and
November 2017;
• Revisit the contract entered into with BSWMI and make a compromise on
how to lessen the charges or find a way on how to take advantage of the
maximum 20 cubic meters per truck load of waste that could be dumped at
the landfill in order to minimize some expenses such as fuel and repairs
and maintenance of dump truck and the corresponding variable expenses
per trip (payment for passway and disinfectant);
• See to it that only residual waste will be dumped in BSWMI in order to
lessen the cost of dumping in the landfill;
• Ensure that the bio-composting facility donated by the Bureau of Soil and
Water Management will be useful in the Solid Waste Management in
coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Office, assign operator/s that
will undergo training on its proper use and look also on its technical side
(such as electrical consumption, availability of technician and parts in case
repair will be necessary, etc.) before final acceptance;
• Coordinate with the BAC to expedite the procurement of plastic shredder
and plastic densifier if they are still necessary for carrying-out the
Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan;
• Enforce the use of eco-bag and other environment-friendly practices
embodied in Section 7 of Ordinance No. 10, series 2014 in coordination
with the Supervising Administrative Officer of Public Market and newly
elected barangay officials; and
• Submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team.
Likewise, the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board oversee the
implementation of the approved Solid Waste Management Plan. Also, the Municipal
Budget Officer ensure that only awarded contracts will be obligated in the registry.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
• Municipal Treasurer to open a special depository account for the
Environmental User’s Fee (EUF) for easy monitoring of collection and
compliance with Ordinance No. 07 of 2016 on the proper sharing and
utilization of funds;
• Municipal Accountant to strictly comply with Section 313 of RA 7160 and
Section 110 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Maintain and
provide complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the preparation of financial
statements on the operation of the Tourism Office to help Management in
its decision making; and
• Municipal Administrator to immediately organize a committee that will be
responsible in policy-making and will oversee the proper
implementation/utilization of the Educational Fund Program so that only
qualified/deserving students will be benefited.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Heads of different
Offices in coordination with MPDC as the lead man to:
• plan for the immediate action/measures to address the seeming delay in
the operation and actual usage of the above enumerated projects. Enhance
policies and procedures to ensure that the funds are effectively utilized and
serve the purpose for which these funds were appropriated.
• see to it that the projects had been properly coordinated with the
barangay/target recipients and equip them with the necessary knowledge
and skills for them to be ready to operate/utilize the projects in
accordance with its intended purpose.
• provide the Audit Team with report of action taken for the aforementioned
unutilized projects to avoid wastage of government funds that may lead to
audit disallowance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
(cid:1) Municipal Engineer to:
• use the PERT/CPM analysis in estimating the actual number of
working days required to complete the project and in granting
extensions;
• conduct strict compliance to detailed engineering activities and close
monitoring of projects to avoid delays on its implementation; and
• submit copy of Statement of Work Accomplished for unperformed
portion signed by the Municipal Engineer and duly acknowledged by
the contractor on the number of days delayed;
(cid:1) Human Resource Management Officer to make a study for possible
creation of a regular plantilla position under the Office of the Municipal
Engineer so that additional personnel will assist the Municipal Engineer in
the conduct of detailed engineering and monitoring of project
implementation;
(cid:1) MPDC/BAC Chairman to ensure that the programs/projects/activities are
properly planned before undergoing bidding process to avoid problems
during the implementation stage that may cause delay and to assist the
Municipal Engineer on project monitoring.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC) and Technical Working Group (TWG) to:
• Submit justification on the failure of the members of the BAC and TWG to
conduct proper and correct procedures in the bid evaluation and post-
qualification stages that resulted in the submission of inaccurate NFCC due
to the non-validation of the contractor’s list of on-going projects/awarded but
not yet started and the award of contracts in spite non-compliance with the
documentary requirements.
• Check if the Municipality’s on-going projects with the bidder with Lowest
Calculated Bid (LCB) has a negative slippage of at least 15% in any one
project or a negative slippage of at least 10% in each of 2 or more contracts
in order to disqualify the contractor in the award;
• Scrutinize the contractor’s sufficiency for manpower and equipment on all
projects to be awarded and not on individual project only in cases where
the contractor is the lowest bidder in several projects which have the same or
overlapping implementation period. This is in order to require contractor to
augment its manpower and/or equipment to be utilized in the project
awarded to it or disqualify it from obtaining several award or contract, and
• Conduct the required processes and procedures in all the stages of the
bidding in the procurement of infrastructure and strictly comply with all the
documentary requirements mandated by RA 9184. Non-compliance of any
of the required documents by the prospective bidders shall be a cause for
automatic disqualification in the eligibility and award of contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor directs the:
• Bids and Awards Committee Chairman to provide explanation for the
inconsistencies in the different phases of the procurement process and to
immediately submit all the lacking documents to the Auditor concerned to
check the propriety of the award of contracts and for further evaluation of
the transaction to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit; and
• Municipal Accountant to check the completeness of required documents
before approval of the claim.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC, its Secretariat
and TWG members to provide explanation on the inconsistencies on the
documentation noted. Likewise, for new members of the BAC, Secretariat and
TWG, require them to attend procurement training or capacity development
program to orient and update them and to carry-out their tasks efficiently and
effectively.

We recommended that the management stop the practice of deducting
personal loans contracted by the employees in their personal capacity and stop
from acting as collecting and remitting agent of unauthorized institution.
Likewise, strictly comply with the GAA provision that monthly take home pay
of employee shall not be lower than Four Thousand Pesos (₱4,000.00).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to make representation with the GSIS with regards to the
Extra-Hazard Pay and if determined that there is no need to provide
government share for the same, stop the practice and revert the amount of
P89,263.34 to Unappropriated Surplus. Also, the Municipal Accountant to
countercheck the payroll prepared by the HRMO for the accuracy of loan
deductions before processing payments of salaries. Likewise, designate new
eRF Handler/official liaison officer who will handle GSIS matters.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the honorable members of
the Sanggunian include among it stop priorities the construction of a building for
the safekeeping and accommodation of the increasing volume of documents
generated by the various departments/offices of the Municipality, particularly
those for submission to the audit team, with consideration on the preservation
period of ten (10) years as mandated by law.

observations.managementAction: The GSO Partially implemented the inventory taking and prepared the RPCPPE which was not submitted on time to the Auditor. (b) The General Services Officer, after the preparation of said RPCPPE, reconcile with that of the Accounting records and exert effort for the disposal of all unserviceable properties; obtain documents of the donated vehicles from PCSO/donor agency, for immediate recording by the Municipal Accountant in the PPE accounts; c. The Municipal Accountant and the designated GSO maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, to facilitate the reconciliation of both records; and d. Cause the titling of the parcel of land in favor of the Municipality.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work to be done at the same time as BAC Secretary and at the same time as GSO, report preparation was not done on time.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Municipal Assessor to copy furnish the Municipal Treasurer of its monthly copy of assessment report; b. Municipal Treasurer to provide the Municipal Accountant a complete list of delinquent taxpayers as of December 31, 2016. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the correct Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor provided the Municipal Treasurer the monthly assessment report. The Municipal Treasurer provided the Municipal Accountant with a complete list of delinquent taxpayers as of December 31, 2016. Thereafter, the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries to recognize the correct Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables in the books of accounts.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant draw the necessary adjusting entries on the current year only. The RPT was set-up only backed-up with a soft copy of certified list of taxpayers while the SET was overstated.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the missing disbursement vouchers and its related supporting documents to the Auditor and to strictly comply with the cited law, rules and regulations relative to the timely submission of documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.managementAction: The GSO prepared partial disbursement vouchers and submitted the same time as GSO, report preparation was not done on time. The Accounting Office was required to submit the missing disbursement vouchers and supporting documents to the Auditor and to strictly comply with the cited law, rules and regulations relative to the timely submission of documents to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous work to be done at the same time. Some vouchers became stale. Stale checks were returned to the general fund as per JEV No. 3239 dated September 30, 2016 in the amount of ₱11,500.00. The Office of the Municipal Treasurer was required to make transmittal letter as to the specific voucher that were submitted to the Accounting Office to be able to track down list of voucher that were already claimed or unclaimed.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Local School Board to consider the enumerated programs/projects as implemented by Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 as implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars and to make an appeal to reconsideration on the disbursements that did not fall within the primary purpose and refund the amount of ₱1,020,711.61.
observations.managementAction: The Supervising Administrative Officer requested a reconsideration from the Local School Board. The Local School Board presented their appeal to the Department concerned and they are awaiting response.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor: a. See to it that the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan of Municipality’s Dumpsite will be fully implemented; b. Coordinate with the Management of Bauan Solid Waste Management, Inc. (BSWMI) for possible utilization of accreditation fee for October and November 2017; c. Revisit the contract entered into with BSWMI and make a compromise on how to lessen the charges or find a way on how to take advantage of the maximum 20 cubic meters per truck load of waste that could be dumped at the landfill in order to minimize some expenses such as fuel and repairs and maintenance of dump truck and the corresponding variable expenses per trip (payment for passway and disinfectant); d. See to it that only residual waste will be dumped in BSWMI in order to lessen the cost of dumping in the landfill; e. Ensure that the bio-composting facility donated by the Bureau of Soil and Water Management will be useful in the Solid Waste Management in coordination with the Municipal Agriculture Office, assign operator/s that will undergo training on its proper use and look also on its technical side (such as electrical consumption, availability of technician and parts in case repair will be necessary, etc.) before final acceptance; f. Coordinate with the BAC to expedite the procurement of plastic shredder and plastic densifier if they are still necessary for carrying-out the Municipality’s Solid Waste Management Plan; g. Enforce the use of eco-bag and other environment-friendly practices embodied in Section 7 of Ordinance No. 10, series 2014 in coordination with the Supervising Administrative Officer of Public Market and newly elected barangay officials; and h. Submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team. Likewise, the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board oversee the implementation of the approved Solid Waste Management Plan. Also, the Municipal Budget Officer ensure that only awarded contracts will be obligated in the registry.
observations.managementAction:
The MENRO replied that: - The Municipality’s dumpsite is already closed and rehabilitated through the financial assistance from DENR-EMB; - Coordination with the BSWMI for the possible utilization of accreditation fee for October and November 2017 was already done, however, it cannot be adjusted anymore since the allowed payment scheme, at the least, is only per quarterly basis; - Revisit of contract entered into and possible compromise on how to lessen the charges of BSWMI will be brought to the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board for review; - The MENR Office will strengthen its “No segregation, No collection policy” to see to it that only residual waste is dumped in BSWMI; - Coordination with the Municipal Agriculturist will be undertaken to enjoy the maximum benefit the BSWM donated equipment will offer in the standpoint of economy and efficiency; - Close coordination with the BAC will be done in order to expedite the procurement of plastic shredder and plastic densifier; - Full enforcement of Ordinance No. 10, series 2014 shall take effect upon the assumption of new set of barangay officials this coming July 2018; and - The Acting MENRO shall submit the documents required by the Audit Team.
Supervising Administrative Officer of the Public Market said that they initiated and promote the use of eco-bags, put tarpaulins and posters, gave notices to stallholders and trained market security personnel to orient them on fines and penalties.
observations.reason: Due to insufficiency of local fund, the rehabilitation of open dumpsite was implemented using local fund. The financial assistance from the DENR-EMB amounting to ₱1,877,862.47 was received by the Municipality on April 2018. There is ongoing work and some target programs/projects of CY 2016 were not yet achieved as of December 31, 2017.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all the mechanisms and processes for the implementation of Magna Carta of Women thru establishment of GAD Database; submission of GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Planning and Development Office and DILG Regional Office; and gender mainstreaming in the local development plans are in place. Likewise, ensure that the reports being prepared and submitted to the Auditor such as GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report corresponds with the Annual Budget and Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO).
observations.managementAction: The MPDC replied that the GAD Database was not yet established and they are using the un-updated CBMS 2009. They are now following the provisions of the joint circular and are preparing required reports though some entries in the GAD Accomplishment Report correspond with the RAAO. The Municipal GAD Focal Point Person will see to it that the reports being prepared and submitted to the Auditor corresponds with the Annual Budget and RAAO.
observations.reason: Some of the reports being prepared and submitted were not prepared on time. The GAD Database was not yet established and they are using un-updated CBMS 2009.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor: a. create a team headed by the Human Resource Officer to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on the actual workloads and manpower requirements for every office that needs them; b. cause the immediate implementation of the projects to avoid reduction of units that could be purchased by the amount granted which may be due to inflation and maintain a close coordination with the BAC for the procurement of various requirements in projects such as training to avoid the occasion of advance delivery by the supplier due to seasonal availability of farmers/fisherfolks ; and c. require refund of ₱20,000.00 from unqualified personnel who received the portion.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer complied and the GAD Plan and GAD Accomplishment Report were prepared. The Municipal Mayor required a study and refund where applicable. The Municipal Office requested refund from unqualified personnel who received the portion.
observations.reason: Some of the projects/activities were implemented but the refund process was still ongoing.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (MDRRMO)-OIC to prepare the LDRRMFIP in accordance with the prescribed format of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and include under a separate caption the projects and activities charged to unexpended LDRRMF of previous years; the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF be prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and submitted to the Auditor; and that the Municipal Budget Officer include in his continuing appropriations the unobligated balance of Capital Outlay and make necessary correction on the understatement of ₱350,000.00.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant gave the copy LDRRMFIP format to the MDRRM Office and instructed them to coordinate with MPDC as to the allocation of previous years disaster fund and to allocate this for mitigation, prevention and preparedness program. The Municipal Budget Officer said that the continuing appropriation for the procurement of Office Equipment of P67,200.00 and IT Equipment of P77,000.00 will be re-appropriated for the project related to disaster supported by Supplemental Annual Investment Plan. The amount of P425,000.00 for the purchase of rubber boat with engine will be included in the Continuing Appropriation Balance 2018.
observations.reason: The Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF was prepared and certified correct but was not regularly submitted; the LDRRMFIP format was given to MDRRMO but not yet fully in prescribed format; continuing appropriation understatement was being addressed.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the provision of the aforesaid PPSAS by classifying the current and non-current portion of long-term loans payable as part of the non-current liabilities and presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within a year from the end of the current reporting period as current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant complied by classifying the non-current portion of loans and presenting the portion due within a year as current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned offices to expedite the implementation of BuB projects so that target recipients could enjoy its benefits; require the contractor to make good of defects/failures in their completed projects and post warranty security; determine exact amount of LDV payment and make corresponding accounting adjustment; and ensure immediate implementation of projects and coordination with the partner agencies.

For the water system, the contractor failed to make good defects and post warranty security. The Municipality was coordinating with concerned agencies and including necessary allocations in its budget to address issues.
observations.reason: Some funds for BuB projects were implemented; some projects not fully completed and contractor defects not yet rectified. Delays and defaults noted.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor: a. create a team headed by the Human Resource Officer to undertake a formal study in improving the revenue of the market and act upon the various causes of losses; accept new lessees for unoccupied stalls; institute actions for intensive collection of delinquencies; require the Supervising Administrative Officer (Market Officer-In-Charge) to maintain a ledger of every stallholder; and require BPLO and Municipal Treasurer to monitor permit and license for businesses; b. make a feasibility study for the operation of the slaughterhouse and examine possible new location; and c. make a study for other possible use of the old slaughterhouse located at the dumpsite to avoid biodegradables disallowance in audit.

The Municipality is looking for new location and allocated funds for purchase of a lot for the slaughterhouse. The old slaughterhouse is being used by the Municipal Agriculturist for biodegradable facility.
observations.reason: Some measures undertaken but full improvement hindered by workload of staff; delinquency collection and full utilization of slaughterhouse still ongoing; Municipality is funding purchase of lot for slaughterhouse.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor enjoin the Municipal Engineer to: a. prepare the Approved Budget for the Contract (ABC) in accordance with DPWH Order No. 22, Series of 2015; b. charge liquidated damages against contractors who failed to complete projects on time; c. display tarpaulin signboards at project locations to inform the public; and d. ensure projects comply with program of works.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Engineer started preparations to apply Detailed Engineering and Unit Price Analysis (DUPA) in the concreting of roads and accepted deficiencies; contractors were charged liquidated damages in some instances and recoveries made. Tarpaulins/signboards were displayed for visited projects; some facilities deteriorating due to non-use.
observations.reason: Due to lack of manpower the Municipal Engineer was not able to use DUPA in all infrastructure projects. Some projects exceeded timelines and some facilities are deteriorating.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor require: a. all members of the BAC to be on a 'jury duty' type of procurement assignment from opening until Notice of Award is issued to complete procurement at the earliest possible time; b. search for other possible suppliers of foods/catering services in the locality to avoid monopoly; c. invite three (3) observers to sit in bidding proceedings; post the Notice of Award in PhilGEPS; adopt standard forms and Standard Bidding Documents for procurement of infrastructure projects; and d. Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing claims without complete supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The BAC tried to complete procurement processes at the earliest possible time but there were isolated cases where the process exceeded three months due to other priorities. The BAC is trying to search for other suppliers and invite observers. The Municipal Accountant stopped processing claims with incomplete supporting documents and is coordinating with the Audit Team to comply with documentary requirements.
observations.reason: Some procurement processes exceeded three months due to scheduling and prioritization; some required documents were lacking and being coordinated with the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Mayor create a group headed by the Human Resource Officer to undertake a thorough study and evaluation on the actual workloads and manpower requirements for every office that needs them, and to augment manpower where necessary and minimize unnecessary jobs employees.

observations.recommendation: that the: Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group submit the lacking documents and ensure compliance with IRR of RA 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001; invite observers; post Notice of Award at PhilGEPS; adopt Standard Bidding Documents; and Municipal Accountant refrain from processing claims without complete supporting documentation.
observations.managementAction: The BAC is now trying to submit the lacking documents and adopt standard procurement documents. The Accounting Office stopped processing claims with incomplete supporting documents and is coordinating with the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: that: a. observations.recommendation: that the Municipal Accountant reconcile the municipal records with the Provincial Accountant and settle discrepancies between the balances of reciprocal accounts so that the Municipality will be able to present the correct balances of inter-agency receivables and payables in the financial statements; request copies of JEVs from the Provincial Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately submit the missing disbursement vouchers and its related supporting documents to the Auditor and to strictly comply with the cited law, rules and regulations relative to the timely submission of accounts to avoid suspension/disallowance in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Local School Board to consider the programs/projects enumerated in Section 272 of Republic Act 7160 as implemented by DECS, DBM and DILG Joint Circulars No. 01-B dated June 25, 2001, No. 01-A dated March 14, 2000 and No. 01 dated April 14, 1998, in the preparation of its budget and refrain from charging non-priority expenditures against the Special Education Fund. Refund the amount of ₱1,020,711.61.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and other officials:
Strictly implement and cause the immediate and effective rehabilitation of  the open  dumpsite and eventually the construction and  maintenance  of sanitary landfill  in accordance with RA 9003;
Direct the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to closely monitor the implementation of the 10-Year Solid Waste Management Plan and the Safe Closure and Rehabilitation Plan of the Dumpsite; and
Enjoin the MENRO, PSTMO, Market Administrator and barangay officials to work hand in hand for the enforcement of Municipal Ordinance No. 10 series of 2014.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a) ensure that all the mechanisms and processes for gender mainstreaming are in place for the implementation of Magna Carta of Women thru establishment of GAD Database, b) ensure  timely submission of GAD Plan and Budget to the Provincial Planning and Development Office to ensure alignment of the municipal GAD PPAs with the priorities of the province and then from the PPDO to the DILG Regional Offices for its review and approval and mainstreaming of gender perspectives in the local development plans. Moreso,  the Focal Point Person see to it that the reports being prepared and submitted to the Auditor such as GAD Plan and Budget and GAD Accomplishment Report corresponds  with the Annual Budget and Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO).

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Health Officer to strictly adhere to the proper disposition and allocation of Per Family Payment Rate (PFPR) in accordance with PhilHealth Advisory No. 06-01-2012. Likewise, require the refund of ₱20,000.00 from unqualified personnel who received the portion of the five percent of PFPR intended for non-health professionals/staff, including volunteers and community members of health team.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer prepare the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) in accordance with the prescribed format in COA Circular No. 2012-002. Likewise, if there are projects and activities charged to the unexpended LDRRMF of previous years, such shall be included in the LDRRMFIP under a separate caption.

We recommended that the Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF be prepared and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant using the prescribed format and submit same on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the LDRRMC and Local Development Council (LDC). Also, the Municipal Treasurer effect the transfer in the Special Trust Fund of the ₱18,280.00 deficiency in the amount previously transferred for the unexpended LDRRMF. Likewise, the Municipal Budget include in his continuing appropriations the Construction of Mini-Dam- Purok 4, Brgy. Kapito amounting to ₱350,000.00.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant provide disclosure of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statement thereafter.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant adhere strictly to the provision of the aforesaid PPSAS by classifying the non-current portion of long-term loans payable as part of the non-current liabilities and presenting the portion of loans due for settlement within a year from the end of the current reporting period as current liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to review in detail all entries made on the Due from/to Other Funds particularly those made in the General Fund and Trust Fund and check  any unrecorded transaction/s in its  reciprocal account/s.  Draw the Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to make necessary adjustments to present the correct balance of the accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned offices to expedite the implementation of the BuB projects so that the target recipients could enjoy its benefits and also in pursuance of the Millennium Development Goal of National Government of reducing poverty in the country.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to determine the exact amount of its default with LDV Construction. On the other hand, require the LDV Construction to make good of the defects/failures in their completed project and to post a warranty security before causing the full payment. Moreover, before the turn-over of the water system project to the barangay or to a cooperative, the Municipal officials should provide the necessary assistance that will ensure the sustainability of its operation and the realization of the main goal of providing potable water to its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant use the Advances to Contractors account when paying the 15% advances to contractors at its gross amount. Likewise, make the necessary adjustment on the erroneous recording of the transfer of LGU counterpart from General Fund to Trust Fund by debiting Due to National Government Agencies and crediting the Transfers from General Fund of LGU Counterpart/Equity Share by ₱718,500.00. Moreso, expedite the implementation of the projects and charge the contractor with liquidated damages in case of delay.

We recommended that the Municipality abide on the stipulation of the MOA that they will provide the “Supplemental Project” and coordinate with the ECCD Council for other provisions such as furniture and fixtures, equipment and learning materials and for the training of Child Development Teacher (CDT) in order for the NCDC to operate in accordance to its intended purpose.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) see to it that the procurement of the requirements for the conduct of training is done on a manner that will ensure the availability of all requirements on the target date and strictly adhere to the provision of RA 9184.
Likewise, the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Agriculturist to cause the immediate implementation of the projects to avoid reduction of units that could be purchased by the amount granted which may be due to inflation and maintain a close coordination with the BAC for the procurement of various requirements in projects such as training to avoid the occasion of advance delivery by the supplier due to seasonal availability of farmers/fisherfolks.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the  Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to take up the unrecorded properties and completed infrastructure project in the books and its corresponding depreciation to reflect  fairly  the Municipality’s Property, Plant and Equipment  and Government Equity.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
All members of BAC to be on a “jury duty” type of assignment until the Notice of Award is issued in order to complete the entire procurement process at the earliest possible time; and
Search for other possible suppliers of foods/catering services in the locality to avoid monopoly that will defeat competitive bidding.

We recommended that the Municipality strictly observe provisions of Article 210 to 213 and 215 of RA 7160 and Section 3.4 of National Budget Circular No. 563 April 22, 2016.  Contributions to leagues which are not included in the preceding articles are not government expenses and should not be charged against the funds of the municipality. In addition, the previously mentioned budget circular specifically disallows individual membership fees paid to private organizations.
